results.possibleIdentity: Robert Maldonado (1936–2025) was a design electrical engineer active in New York during the late 1960s and early 1970s. Born on July 17, 1936, in Ponce, Puerto Rico, he moved to New York City with his family in 1944. He likely graduated from Manhattan College (Class of 1959) and began a career in electrical engineering and instrumentation. Maldonado worked for Philips Electronics Instruments (PEI), a division of North American Philips based in the New York area (Mount Vernon, NY), and was also associated with Pennwalt Corporation's S.S. White Dental division. His technical expertise spanned electrical engineering, mathematics, computer systems, and digital instrumentation. He was an inventor on several patents related to medical and dental technology, specifically focused on reducing X-ray exposure for patients through advanced circuit design and digital control systems. His specialized knowledge included tape recording, integrated circuits, and transistors. He was recorded as an engineer in the Technical Services index of Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.), an organization that paired artists with technical experts for collaborative projects. In his later years, he moved to California, where he passed away in September 2025.
